raw.population: Elderly N: 37 (Russian) Age: 62.9±6.18 WT: 77±11.64 Young N:35 (Russian) Age: 34.1±7.8 WT: 69.01±12.3
raw.objective: “can be used to plan individualized CBZ dosage regimens in epileptic elderly postinduction patients on chronic CBZ monotherapy. It is also especially well-suited for use with the new ‘multiple model’ design of dosage regimens, to achieve desired therapeutic target goals with maximum precision”
raw.covariates: Tested: age, gender, weight, height, diagnosis, creatinine and comedication. Included: WT on Vd
raw.observations: Differences between adults and elderly were found, and adjusting dosing was recommended. The analysis is a combination of model-based and empirical approach. It seems that CBZ dose was not tested as covariate on clearance, but on the concentration/dose ratio. Although the authors claim that this model could have important practical consequences for CBZ use in elderly, it is unclear how well the model predicts on the individual level without using a bayesian approach with TDM.
raw.equations: Ka=theta1*exp(eta1) Vd=theta2*WT*exp(eta2) Ki=theta3*exp(eta3) Error model not reported Ki is assumed to be the metabolic constant, as elimination is assumed to be almost exclusively hepatic
raw.parameter_values: theta1=0.61 theta2=0.81 theta3=0.04 omega1=0.91 omega2=0.785 omega3=0.63
raw.validation: No / Yes, compared to predictions based on a previously built model.

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
